descriptions.allow_merge_commit: Prevents merge commits on the default branch, enforcing a clean bisectable history where every change is squash-merged or rebase-merged.
descriptions.allow_squash_merge: Allows squash-merging pull requests into a single commit, keeping the default branch history linear and easy to read.
descriptions.allow_rebase_merge: Allows rebase-merging pull requests for a linear history without an extra merge commit.
descriptions.allow_auto_merge: Disabled so pull requests cannot auto-merge without an explicit human action, even when all checks pass.
descriptions.delete_branch_on_merge: Automatically deletes merged branches to prevent stale branch accumulation.
descriptions.allow_update_branch: Allows contributors to rebase their PR branch on the latest base, reducing stale-base merge conflicts.
descriptions.web_commit_signoff_required: Requires a DCO sign-off on all commits made through the GitHub web UI, matching the CLI requirement and ensuring contributor agreement for every change.
descriptions.secret_scanning: Scans commits for secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) and alerts the repository owner on detection, reducing the exposure window of accidentally committed credentials.
descriptions.secret_scanning_push_protection: Blocks pushes that contain detected secrets before they reach GitHub — prevents credential leaks at push time, not just after the fact.
descriptions.dependabot_security_updates: Automatically opens pull requests to update dependencies with known CVEs, keeping the dependency graph free of publicly-disclosed vulnerabilities.
descriptions.vulnerability_alerts: Alerts the repository owner when a dependency has a known security vulnerability, giving early warning of exposure in the dependency graph.
descriptions.default_workflow_permissions: Limits GITHUB_TOKEN to read-only scope by default, requiring workflows to explicitly request write access and reducing blast radius of a compromised workflow.
descriptions.can_approve_pull_request_reviews: Allows workflow runs to approve pull requests, required for automated release bots that must unblock their own PRs.
